name: TA-65 meta-analysis reports telomere movement without frailty or inflammation improvement
summary: Review-level TA-65 synthesis strengthens the biomarker read but reinforces the gap between telomere length and useful human outcomes.
statement: A 2025 systematic review and meta-analysis of 8 randomized TA-65 trials reported leukocyte telomere-length improvement but no significant frailty or inflammation improvement, with only short-term safety evidence.
population_or_model: 8 randomized controlled trials with 750 participants and mean participant age of 63.3 years
time_horizon: Safety synthesis covered up to 12 months in the abstracted review
quantitative_note: Telomere-length SMD 0.47 (95% CI 0.31 to 0.62); frailty SMD 0.09, p = 0.15; CRP/IL-6 SMD -0.11, p = 0.07; mild gastrointestinal adverse events 12.4%.
caveats: Industry-funded trials reported larger telomere effects in subgroup analysis.
No severe adverse events or oncogenesis over 12 months does not settle longer-term cancer risk.
The review supports a biomarker-stage read rather than a claim that human function improves.
